text: Do you have a favorable or unfavorable view of the United States?
source: Pew Research Center Global Attitudes, Spring 2024 (Germany N=1,008)
notes: VERIFIED from raw data. 50.5% favorable / 49.5% unfavorable. Near-even split reflects post-Trump ambivalence about the US. AfD voters expected strongly unfavorable; Greens/FDP expected somewhat favorable. Tests transatlantic sentiment dimension — NOT in calibration set.

text: Do you have a favorable or unfavorable view of the United Nations?
source: Pew Research Center Global Attitudes, Spring 2024 (Germany N=1,008)
notes: VERIFIED from raw data. 63.8% favorable. Multilateral institution support — tests Institutional Trust dimension. AfD/BSW expected unfavorable; mainstream parties expected favorable. Tests whether IT dimension generalises beyond NATO/EU.

text: Do you have a favorable or unfavorable opinion of the Social Democratic Party (SPD)?
source: Pew Research Center Global Attitudes, Spring 2024 (Germany N=1,008)
notes: VERIFIED from raw data. 39.0% favorable. Center-left — AfD voters strongly unfavorable; CDU voters split (Grand Coalition rivals but not enemies); Greens/Left broadly favorable. Tests party-partisan crossover prediction.

text: Do you have a favorable or unfavorable opinion of the Free Democratic Party (FDP)?
source: Pew Research Center Global Attitudes, Spring 2024 (Germany N=1,008)
notes: VERIFIED from raw data. 33.8% favorable — lowest approval of any mainstream party post-traffic light coalition collapse. CDU/FDP broadly favorable; SPD/Left/AfD/BSW unfavorable. Tests Individualism dimension: FDP is the neoliberal market party.

text: How much confidence do you have in U.S. President Joe Biden to do the right thing regarding world affairs?
source: Pew Research Center Global Attitudes, Spring 2024 (Germany N=1,008)
notes: VERIFIED from raw data. 63.7% some/lot of confidence — mirror image of the Trump question (de12, 86.7% no confidence). AfD voters expected D (anti-establishment); Greens/SPD/CDU expected B. Tests whether the Biden/Trump contrast is internally consistent with de12.

text: How much confidence do you have in Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to do the right thing regarding world affairs?
source: Pew Research Center Global Attitudes, Spring 2024 (Germany N=1,008)
notes: VERIFIED from raw data. 55.1% some/lot confidence — significant D (26.6%) reflects AfD/BSW Russia-sympathetic cluster. Complements Russia view (de02) and NATO (de06). Tests whether East German/AfD personas correctly cluster at D. Novel test of the geopolitics cluster coherence.

text: How much confidence do you have in French President Emmanuel Macron to do the right thing regarding world affairs?
source: Pew Research Center Global Attitudes, Spring 2024 (Germany N=1,008)
notes: VERIFIED from raw data. 75.4% some/lot confidence — broadly cross-partisan in Germany. AfD/BSW expected lower; Greens/SPD/CDU expected B. Tests general European leadership confidence. A high B (58.5%) makes this a useful baseline — not polarizing, tests model's ability to avoid extreme hedging.
